summary: tryNewTag returns a 3rd error variant {kind: 'invalid-tag'} that is not part of the canonical TagError type contract.
defect: The canonical Tag Smart Constructor type contract at docs/domain/code/ts/src/shared/value-objects.ts:43 declares TagError = { kind: 'empty' } | { kind: 'only-whitespace' } — only two variants. The local tryNewTag implementation at try-new-tag.ts lines 19-22 declares TagValidationError with three variants (empty, only-whitespace, invalid-tag) and rejects any normalized string not matching /^[a-z0-9-]+$/. This invents a rejection path that the canonical type contract does not authorize, and silently constrains the Tag character set to ASCII alphanumerics + hyphen — which behavioral-spec.md Open Question 4 explicitly defers to the Smart Constructor's actual policy. Because parseFilterInput at parse-filter-input.ts:25 always rewraps any tryNewTag failure as { kind: 'invalid-tag', raw: rawTag }, the public ParseFilterInput contract still matches REQ-003. However, downstream code that consumes tryNewTag directly via index.ts (which does export tryNewTag) sees an error type incompatible with the canonical TagError, and any future Japanese / non-ASCII tag from REQ-002 is silently rejected with no spec basis. This is a non-public-contract divergence from the source-of-truth type contract.
evidence: docs/domain/code/ts/src/shared/value-objects.ts line 43 reads `export type TagError = { kind: "empty" } | { kind: "only-whitespace" };`. promptnotes/src/lib/domain/apply-filter-or-search/try-new-tag.ts lines 19-22 declare a local TagValidationError with `| { kind: "invalid-tag" }`. Line 54 returns `err({ kind: 'invalid-tag' })` for any string that survives normalization but contains characters outside [a-z0-9-]. promptnotes/src/lib/domain/apply-filter-or-search/index.ts line 3 re-exports tryNewTag publicly.
suggested_fix: Either (a) rename the local error type to TagValidationError and document explicitly that parseFilterInput is the only intended consumer (do not re-export tryNewTag from index.ts so the type drift stays internal); or (b) drop the /^[a-z0-9-]+$/ check and only return the two canonical TagError variants, deferring character-set policy to a future spec amendment per Open Question 4. Option (b) better matches the canonical contract and behavioral-spec.md REQ-002 which only mandates trim/lowercase/strip-#.
